1001.Name: About Me - I
1001.DictionaryDes:
According to Airona, she rescued me from a Goblin's cauldron.
But I have no memory of any of this...
The Number 71 mentioned in that strange dream... breaking the shackles of civilizational development... is that a memory from my past?
Why have I lost all my memories?

1002.Name: Luno
1002.DictionaryDes:
A currency widely used on the Continent of Magna, managed and minted by the Bapharian Creed.
The minting material is a precious bullion containing Engram.
The craftsmanship of Luno coins is so superb that they are nearly impossible to counterfeit.

1003.Name: Operation Luno!
1003.DictionaryDes:
To pay off the lodging fees at The Double-Faced Coin and to repay Airona for saving my life.
I plan to work with Airona to earn enough money.
Airona said she has a way to earn enough Luno within one month to repay the tavern owner, with a surplus to enjoy delicacies in Asterleeds.
Since I'm in an unfamiliar place... I might as well give it a try!
Operation Luno begins!

1004.Name: Teleportal
1004.DictionaryDes:
An ancient Bapharian Legacy that can teleport a creature touching it to another Teleportal along the Engram Line. The principle is not yet clear.
It is a very common "public facility" with widespread application.
Teleportals are set up in various settlements and important locations. They cannot teleport large quantities of goods, only a small amount of personal supplies.

1005.Name: The Double-Faced Coin
1005.DictionaryDes:
An old tavern located at Entertainment District in the north of the city, though calling it an tavern is a bit of a stretch.
This is because The Double-Faced Coin offers a wide range of services, including dining, lodging, and specialty drinks. Due to its central location at Entertainment District, it is very well-known.
People nearby love to go in for a couple of drinks when they are free.
The owner, Wilbert, is a kind-hearted uncle, but for some reason, he speaks with a Byrnhald accent.

1006.Name: Airona's Identity - I
1006.DictionaryDes:
"The renowned legend", "the pride of the Pioneer Bureau", "the legendary adventurer", "the brilliant star of Asterleeds"...
To receive such honors, Airona's identity seems to be not as simple as I imagined.

1007.Name: Seabreeze Festival
1007.DictionaryDes:
The Council established an annual city-wide celebration to commemorate the independence of Asterleeds, known as the "Seabreeze Festival."
On the day of the festival, a shower of brilliant colored ribbons falls over Asterleeds. Adults tell their children that this is a blessing from the stars and the sea.

Citizens will place blooming Sea Lilies by their doorsteps and windows to express their wishes for peace.
During the Seabreeze Festival, people also exchange Sea Lilies with each other to express their cherish and blessings.
Various special celebrations, such as poetry gatherings, parades, and the Carnival, are also held in the city.

1008.Name: Tales of the Plains
1008.DictionaryDes:
"Those who step out of the city gates will be devoured by the black fog monsters."
The originally crowded Seabreeze Festival has seen a sharp drop in attendance this time due to the Tales of the Plains. What is the truth behind these tales?

1009.Name: Snow Knight
1009.DictionaryDes:
The Snow Knight is highly skilled. Despite wearing a clumsy Snow Knight suit, they can still flexibly repel monsters and rescue merchants.
Hidden inside the clumsy Snow Knight suit is Tina, a Freedom Fighter of Asterleeds.
Her popularity in the city is comparable to a walking pile of Luno.
However, Tina appearing on the plains in a Snow Knight suit seems to have a hidden reason.

1010.Name: Void Engram
1010.DictionaryDes:
A twisted form of Engram of unknown origin, appearing as black mist cloud.
When Void Engram appears, the Engram in the surrounding space becomes chaotic and extremely dangerous.

Besides, Void Engram and Engram undergo a neutralization reaction, so the only way to dispel it is to inject a large amount of Engram into the contaminated area.

1011.Name: Wind Gate and Wind Field
1011.DictionaryDes:
The intense collision of Winds creates a miraculous phenomenon called a wind field.
Abundant Winds fill the wind field, forming invisible wind gates.

1012.Name: About Me - II
1012.DictionaryDes:
After a brief moment of stun and discomfort, I found my body dissipating into the air like wind-blown flower petals...
In the end, this dissipation seems related to certain events I experienced before, and it is precisely these experiences that caused my memory loss and physical injuries.

1013.Name: The Dissipating Man in Black
1013.DictionaryDes:
We finally tracked the source of the Void Engram to a mysterious person dressed in a pitch-black robe.
It was he who deliberately spread the Void Engram, causing all the strange Engram Anomalys on the plains.

When defeated, the Man in Black knelt on the ground, obsessively whispering, "How can such a grand cause be..." , and then dissipated into black mist.

1014.Name: Pioneer Bureau
1014.DictionaryDes:
A global organization that supports adventurer activities, the excavation of Bapharian Ruins, and the hunting of monsters. The Pioneer Bureau's stance is neutral and does not belong to any country.
The person in charge of the Asterleeds Branch is Miss Valeria, known as "Miss Bright Idea." You can go to her for commissions in the future.

1015.Name: Imagine Research Institute
1015.DictionaryDes:
Founded by legendary artisan Mason Stonecutter, the institute specializes in the research and application of Imagine Seeds.
Mason was once a central figure in the Artisan Guild and pioneered Legacy modification theory, but left after the Guild rejected his vision for Imagine Seeds.

The institute upholds his belief that Imagine Seeds hold potential beyond traditional Legacies, striving to break technical boundaries and explore more creative forms of craftsmanship.
Today, it operates independently as a frontier institution, advancing innovations overlooked by the mainstream.

1016.Name: The Mystery of Tina's Identity
1016.DictionaryDes:
The stolen file revealed that Tina harbors a latent danger, prompting the Council to impose strict containment measures.
Therefore, Tina is restricted to only being able to move within Asterleeds, which is why she disguised herself as the Snow Knight to resolve the crisis on the plains.

1017.Name: Being Watched?
1017.DictionaryDes:
It was a false alarm. It turns out to be Olvera, the commander of the Asterleeds Defense Corps soldiers, who wants to find her teacher, Airona.
That's why she was commissioning people everywhere, asking us to meet in Arena Town.
However, for such a powerful commander to also call Airona her teacher, just how powerful is Airona really...

1018.Name: About Me - III
1018.DictionaryDes:
The Man in Black mentioned that I don't seem to be controlled by the Void Engram.
It seems there is some mysterious power hidden within my body...

1019.Name: Seabreeze Medal and Knighting Ceremony
1019.DictionaryDes:
Awarded personally by the Council, it symbolizes the highest honor for making outstanding contributions to Asterleeds.
Those who receive this honor must be outstanding individuals who have turned the tide in a crisis and brought profound changes to the city.
The Knighting Ceremony is a celebration for the entire city. Citizens gather at Ocean Hill to witness the hero's coronation.
This is not only a recognition of individual merit but also a praise of the spirit of freedom.

Tina has been named this year's recipient of the prestigious Seabreeze Medal.

1020.Name: Tina's Inner Struggle
1020.DictionaryDes:
War made Tina feel powerless. The loss of comrades and the Kanamian tragedy haunted her deeply.
Though called a hero, the Council restricted her freedom as a Seal Watcher.
The Void Engram amplified her pain, pulling her into darkness.

In a crucial moment, I resonated with her Bio-Engram and entered her mind. Together, we faced the pain she had buried-loss, despair, and the freedom she could never reach.

1021.Name: About Me - IV
1021.DictionaryDes:
I appear to possess a special Resonance Power, allowing deep connections with others' Bio-Engram and restoring life touched by the Void Engram. It was this power that let me enter Tina's Mindrealm and bring her back from the edge.
And the source of this power seems to be closely tied to my past...

The fact that Hruga sent me the remaining horn fragments through her followers means she's already set her sights on my Resonance Power secretly.
After all, it's a direct threat to the Void Engram.

1022.Name: Fireworks Festival
1022.DictionaryDes:
During the Seabreeze Festival, at dusk, fireworks are set off on the beach, illuminating the entire sky of Asterleeds.
The firework patterns range from Sea Lilies to cat heads, having everything one could wish for.
The fireworks display creates a dreamlike scene, becoming a signature sight of the Seabreeze Festival.

1023.Name: The Witch's Whisper
1023.DictionaryDes:
The gift inside the box turned out to be... a fragment of Tina's Medal?!

The moment my fingers brushed against the shard, a whisper echoed in my mind-
"Accept my gift. I shall bestow upon thee the blade that can sunder fate itself. "

Moments later, my consciousness began to fade... as if I were drifting into the depths of the Void Engram...

1024.Name: The Fragment of Hruga's Horn
1024.DictionaryDes:
The black keratin shard found in Tina's medal was the very source of the Void Engram at the Knighting Ceremony.
The mist leaked from this fragment, and contact with it disrupts the mind, causing brief loss of control.

This tampered shard was hidden inside a gift box—disguised as a present and delivered to me.
Even "I" couldn't resist its power.
After years of silence, Hruga's shadow has returned to Magna. Perhaps her followers are already working behind the scenes...

1025.Name: Hruga, the Witch of Calamity
1025.DictionaryDes:
Witch Hruga, the spreader of Void Engram, is an extremely dangerous existence.

Three years ago, Hruga was sealed in the deepest part of the continent.
But rumor has it Hruga has recently been trying to break the seal...

1026.Name: Hruga's Seal
1026.DictionaryDes:
The divine seal that holds Hruga's power is called the "Core of Stellarium."
Those who bear it are "Seal Watchers."
There are two Watchers in total—Tina is one of them. That's why the Council assigned guards to protect her.

The recent Tales of the Plains were likely attempts by Hruga's follower, the "person in black," to break the Stellar Seal​.

It becomes clear that the Tales of the Plains, the attack at the ceremony, the person in black...
All these evil acts trace back to the witch Hruga, who now seeks to shatter the Stellar Seal​ on her through her follower, the person in black.

1027.Name: The Spreader of Void Engram
1027.DictionaryDes:
The fragment of Hruga's Horn hidden within the medal confirms that the person in black is a follower of the sealed witch Hruga.

When combined with the ceremony attack, it becomes clear—the true mastermind behind all this evil is the witch Hruga, working through her followers to reclaim her power.
Now, they've set their sights on me-perhaps because they sensed my unusual resistance to the Void Engram.

1028.Name: Revelation
1028.DictionaryDes:
It is said that when Hruga's seal was restored, a revelation emerged:
"The eternal calamity is nigh. Awaken... Bearer of Resonance Power, you shall remember the sign of salvation, and bear the power of all beings to halt the end of fate..."

So far, I am the only one with Resonance Power. This "coincidence" seems to hint that both my past and future are tightly bound to this revelation.

1029.Name: Council Chair and the Council
1029.DictionaryDes:
The highest governing body of Asterleeds consists of 17 councilors and a Council Chair, with members drawn from various sectors and communities.
Asterians value equality and believe the city's future should be shaped by voices from all walks of life.

The current Council Chair is Lamond, a kind and insightful elder.
He entrusted me with uncovering the truth behind the Void Engram in the medal-and granted us generous support, including the privileged Ocean Platinum Card.

1030.Name: The Real Culprit - Dark Mist Legion
1030.DictionaryDes:
The one who tampered with Tina's medal was a craftsman named Ryan-forced into it after the Dark Mist Legion took his family hostage.
This reveals the true culprit: the Dark Mist Legion, agents of Witch Hruga.

Once an elite unit of the Byrnhald Knights, the Legion refused to return after the Independence War.
Instead, they remained near Asterleeds, launching repeated attacks. Their leader, Denvel, a former knight, bears deep hatred toward the city.

1031.Name: The Fake Tycoon Kakater
1031.DictionaryDes:
The "tycoon" Kakater aboard the luxury cruise was just a front, crafted by the infamous swindler "Greenbottle Fly."
He struck a deal with Byrnhald Executive Officer Taltraud: under a stand-in agreement, Taltraud gathered intel in disguise, while the swindler plotted in secret.

As the officer enjoyed the convenience of anonymity, the conman had already siphoned away his assets through clever financial tricks.
Their alliance soon unraveled into a full-blown conspiracy on the high seas.

1032.Name: Operation White Night
1032.DictionaryDes:
The codename given by Council Chair Lamond for the joint operation between Asterleeds and Byrnhald Executive Officer Tatta to annihilate the Dark Mist Legion.
As the saying goes-"Under the blazing white night, darkness is torn asunder and scattered to the winds like fading smoke, as mighty heroes rend the stars from the heavens."

1033.Name: Byrnhald Executive Officer
1033.DictionaryDes:
A special military position in the Duchy of Byrnhald with extremely high authority.
It is said that Byrnhald cannot tolerate the rebellious attitude of the Dark Mist Legion, viewing them as a disgrace to the duchy, and has therefore dispatched the executive officer to eliminate them.

1034.Name: Rumors of the Dark Mist Legion - I
1034.DictionaryDes:
Three years ago, the Dark Mist Legion defected from Byrnhald and repeatedly attacked Asterleeds.
Though most of their forces were destroyed, signs of a comeback have emerged.

Byrnhald, outraged by their betrayal, saw it as a disgrace.
The Knights dispatched Taltraud to eliminate them-especially their leader, Denvel.

1035.Name: The key to defeating the Dark Mist Legion is...
1035.DictionaryDes:
Tatta revealed that the key to defeating Denvel lies with a Dark Mist Legion aide named Jayden.
However, he mysteriously vanished years ago in the Towering Ruin.

Records show that Jayden led an infiltration mission into Asterleeds. After it failed, he fled into the ruins and was never seen again-presumed dead.

1036.Name: Imagine & Imagine Seed
1036.DictionaryDes:
When strong emotions or spiritual elements merge with Engram and become physical, they form what's known as Imagine.

On Regnus, Imagine is a common supernatural force rivaling the Legacy. Users can summon entities, weapons, or empower themselves. Its limits remain unknown.

Imagine is stored in gem-like items called Imagine Seeds, which must be activated. These seeds are hard to destroy and appear randomly-no one can create or predict them.

1037.Name: Rumors of the Dark Mist Legion - II
1037.DictionaryDes:
Denvel, leader of the Dark Mist Legion, refused to accept the war-ending peace treaty. Fueled by revenge, he defied orders and attacked Asterleeds.

After being defeated by Tina and Olvera, he fell into despair and was swayed by the Man in Black, choosing to side with Witch Hruga and use her power to destroy Asterleeds.

It was revenge-and a twisted tribute to his fallen comrades.

1038.Name: Ship Ticket or Intellect
1038.DictionaryDes:
With Denvel on the verge of attacking Asterleeds,
Council Chair Lamond did not force me to stay but gave me a choice:
a dignified departure, or to join the fight against the Dark Mist Legion.

1039.Name: Engram Anomaly
1039.DictionaryDes:
Engram and Void Engram are opposing forces.
Engram maintains life's balance, while Void Engram brings madness and chaos.

Flowing through Engram Lines, Engram nourishes the world.
If blocked by Void Engram, Engram Anomalies occur-unleashing storms, hurricanes, or floods as elemental forces spiral out of control.

1040.Name: Engram Anomaly - Thunderstorm
1040.DictionaryDes:
A massive amount of thunder energy converged into a devastating thunderstorm, mercilessly striking Minsterhorn and causing irreparable damage...
Even more deadly, the lingering thunder energy solidified into a barrier, blocking the way forward and making rescue efforts extremely difficult.

Only by locating the source of the Engram Anomaly can this catastrophe be resolved.

1041.Name: Source of the Engram Anomaly
1041.DictionaryDes:
After an Engram Anomaly occurs due to an element cycle collapse, the collapsed elements will gather in one place.
This gathering point is called the Source.

The Source may appear in powerful creatures or attach itself to special objects, and is usually difficult to find.
Resonating with the Source using my Resonance Power is currently the only way to resolve an Engram Anomaly.

1042.Name: Void Sigil
1042.DictionaryDes:
A lost and forbidden magical formula that can block the flow of Engram Lines, allowing Void Engram to spread.
By controlling "Void Engram" with Hruga's Horn, the magical formula can be activated, thus completing the placement of the sigils.
When all sigils are set, the magical formula will cause a large-scale spread of "Void Engram," leading to a massive Void Cataclysm disaster.

1043.Name: Denvel's True Agenda
1043.DictionaryDes:
There are four Void Sigils: one in the Dragonclaw Ruins, one in Minsterhorn, one in the Monument Statue of the Hero,
and the last one is set within Asterleeds.
The goal of Denvel, the leader of the Dark Mist Legion, is to use the Void Sigils to engulf the entire plain to achieve his revenge.
The only way to undo this is to find a large amount of Engram to neutralize the Void Engram within the sigils, thereby dismantling Void Sigil.

1044.Name: The Legend of the Bapharian Divine
1044.DictionaryDes:
Long ago, the Bapharian Divine who ruled Regnus vanished, causing humanity to lose its technological foundation and fall into decline.
As civilization neared collapse, divine emissaries came forth with the "Oracle."
This gave rise to the Bapharian Creed, guiding humanity's rebirth.

Today, people know the Divine were simply another race resembling humans. Yet most still hold them in deep reverence.

1045.Name: Engram Collector
1045.DictionaryDes:
A very rare type of Legacy that can slowly collect nearby Engram and store it stably within.
Engram collected by an Engram Collector can be stored within it for hundreds of years.
Currently, Engram Collectors have been found in various sizes, with different-sized collectors storing different amounts of Engram.

1046.Name: Kanamia Tribe
1046.DictionaryDes:
Before Asterleeds was founded, the Kanamia Tribe had long lived and thrived on this land.

Unlike modern civilization that relies on Bapharian Legacies, the Kanamians lead simple, peaceful lives in harmony with nature.
They worship their ancestors as Ancestral Spirits, and under their chieftain's leadership, live traditionally through hunting and gathering.
Known for their superb craftsmanship, Kanamian artisans make all daily tools and housing within the tribe.

During the Independence War, the Kanamians fought alongside Asterleeds.
But after the Battle of Kanamia Forest, many lost their homes and families, causing the tribe to grow isolated and cut ties with the outside world.

1047.Name: The Beliefs of Kanamians
1047.DictionaryDes:
Kanamians believe in ancestral spirits, holding that their kin return to the ancestral spirits after death.
The ancestral spirits are the world itself, watching over and protecting the tribe.
But the ancestral spirits cannot communicate directly with Kanamians and can only convey their will through priests.

1048.Name: Rumors of the Forbidden Zone of Kanamia - I
1048.DictionaryDes:
Engram in Kanamia is very abundant. It is said that this may be related to the "Forbidden Land" located within their territory.
It is said that the waters of the Forbidden Zone of Kanamia possess healing abilities, which further suggests that the concentration of Engram there is exceptionally high.

1049.Name: "Green Warbler"
1049.DictionaryDes: The competition codename Rorola prepared for Airona in the Kanamia Tribe's Kanamia Brave trial. It means "a green bird in the forest." It combines Airona's affinity for nature with her bird-like agility in combat.

1050.Name: "Tide Envoy"
1050.DictionaryDes:
The competition codename Rorola prepared for the protagonist in the Kanamia Tribe's Kanamia Brave trial.
It means "a traveler from the sea." It alludes to the protagonist coming from the seaside city of Asterleeds and their future travels across the world.

1051.Name: Kanamia Brave Trial
1051.DictionaryDes:
In the Kanamia Tribe, the Warrior holds key responsibilities: defending the tribe, overseeing hunts, and carrying out administrative duties.
This role is bound by a lifetime tenure and only transitions when the current Warrior can no longer wield a blade due to old age, death in battle, or special circumstances.

Selection trials are strict-only elite members approved by the elders may take part.
These include combat simulations, endurance tests, and tactical evaluations, judged by the current Warrior, Chieftain, and Priest.

Only one Warrior serves at a time. The successor inherits all authority and honor, and any change in office must be jointly decided by the Chieftain, Priest, and the current Warrior.

1052.Name: Rumors of the Forbidden Zone of Kanamia - II
1052.DictionaryDes:
The Forbidden Land, which has been guarded by the Kanamians for generations, is reverently called "The Ancestors' Gift".
According to the tribespeople, it contains the sacred "power of the ancestors", which is the lifeblood of the tribe's survival.
The tribespeople firmly believe that the water in the Forbidden Land can heal wounds and grant strength, a gift from the ancestors to protect Kanamia.

1053.Name: Old Kanamia Tribe
1053.DictionaryDes:
The Kanamians once lived for generations along the shores of Pale Azure Lake, but the Independence War changed everything.
Flames engulfed their homeland, and the tribe's living space shrank drastically.

The damage ran deeper-many lives were lost, and the survivors couldn't return to bury their fallen kin.
Now, the ruins are haunted by twisted Engram Aberrants, forcing the Kanamians to face the nightmare of fighting their once-loved ones if they wish to reclaim it.

That bloodstained land remains an unhealed scar, a constant reminder of the pain left by the war.

1054.Name: Engram Aberrants
1054.DictionaryDes:
An Engram Aberrant is a mutated lifeform born from the fusion of Engram energy and lingering obsessions. It must cling to a physical corpse or specific item to maintain its form.
Commonly known in folklore as "ghosts," "undead," or "spirits",

these beings emerge when strong emotions at death prevent full Bio-Engram dispersal-leaving the spirit linked to Engram energy in a twisted union. Aberrants are surrounded by a natural Engram field, but lacking Bio-Engram, they instinctively attack nearby life to steal it and survive.

Most are mindless echoes of who they once were, but some reports speak of aberrants capable of speech.

1055.Name: Kanamia Forest Skirmish - I
1055.DictionaryDes:
During Asterleeds Independence War, the Kanamia Tribe, located on the border of Asterleeds, joined forces with Asterleeds to resist the Byrnhald army.
A special unit composed of artisans, Kanamia Warriors, and Asterleeds Troopers set up defensive facilities at the border to repel the Byrnhald army's offensive.

Unfortunately, the Byrnhald's attack came earlier than intel predicted. This led to the heavy losses of the joint unit after they were caught in an ambush.

1056.Name: Kanamia Forest Skirmish - II
1056.DictionaryDes:
During the skirmish, Commander Olvera led her subordinates to break out, while also heading to various deployment points to rescue the artisans who were setting up the installations.
However, to protect their homes, the artisans completed the deployment of the defensive facilities with their own lives.

1057.Name: Kanamia Forest Skirmish - III
1057.DictionaryDes:
In the final moments of the battle, High Priestess Yaretsi of the Kanamia Tribe led the last surviving warriors in a brave assault to divert the enemy forces.

Their sacrifice bought Olvera enough time to activate the defense system alone.
United, the Asterleeds soldiers, artisans, and Kanamians repelled the Byrnhald army's advance.

The survivors knew-if the border fell, their homeland would be lost to war.
So they chose to protect it with their lives.

Yet Priestess Yaretsi's will remained. Twisted by obsession, she became an Engram Aberrant, standing at the ruins of Kanamia. Holding the Key to the Forbidden Land, she waits in silence for her people's return...

1058.Name: Engram Anomaly - Hurricane
1058.DictionaryDes:
The Wind, influenced by the Void Sigil, began to gather in large quantities near the Source.
The congested Wind eventually formed a terrifying tornado, which struck the Kanamia Tribe.

The hurricane caused severe damage, destroying the houses of the Kanamia Tribe, uprooting trees, and displacing the tribespeople...

1059.Name: Forbidden Zone of Kanamia
1059.DictionaryDes:
When the gates of the Forbidden Land opened, the truth was astonishing— the so-called "Power of the Ancestors" was actually a massive Engram Collector, far beyond standard priestly models.

These ancient Bapharian Legacies infused Engram into the water, giving it powerful Healing.
The flow gathered below, forming the Sacred Spring worshipped by the Kanamians for generations.

Now, this giant Collector offers new hope— its energy may be enough to cleanse the spreading Void Sigil.

1060.Name: The Spread of Void Sigils
1060.DictionaryDes:
The Man in Black and Denvel used a forbidden Igdias' ritual to amplify the Void Sigil, a technique that causes their spread.
Once spread, the Void Sigil becomes even stronger and is difficult to eliminate in the short term.
Even more dangerous, if multiple Void Sigils are placed in the same area, they will amplify each other, ultimately leading to the entire region being consumed by the Void Engram.

1061.Name: Denvel's Final Assault
1061.DictionaryDes:
The Dark Mist Legion realized that the other Void Sigils were being dispelled, so they launched an all-out assault on the location of the final sigil: Asterleeds.
If Denvel completes the final mark sigil this time, all the sigils will amplify each other, eventually causing the entire plains to be swallowed by the Void Engram.
Therefore, it is imperative to use the massive Engram Collector found in the Forbidden Land of Kanamia to dispel the Void Sigil before he succeeds.

1062.Name: About Me - V
1062.DictionaryDes:
At the moment the final Void Sigil erupted, the violent energy shock completely destroyed the Engram Collector.
In the nick of time, I had to forcibly channel all the remaining Engram from the device into my own body.
With this power, I finally cleared the last Void Sigil, shattering Denvel's conspiracy.

As my consciousness began to fade, a distant yet familiar voice pierced through the darkness:" Please... wake up..."
Is this call an echo of reality, or an awakening of past memories?

1063.Name: Dark Mist Fortress
1063.DictionaryDes:
When the Asterleeds Independence War ended, the Dark Mist Legion refused the recall order and secretly established a military base in a hidden area deep within the plains.
After colluding with Hruga's follower, this fortress became shrouded in dense Void Engram energy, forming a barrier difficult to penetrate.
Now, as the Void Sigils on the plain gradually dissipate, the dark barrier surrounding the fortress has crumbled. The time for a counterattack has finally arrived.

1064.Name: Engram Hub
1064.DictionaryDes:
Engram flows across Planet Regnus in a network of Engram Lines.
The gathering points of this Engram Line network are called Engram Hubs, where the Engram concentration and power are higher.

1065.Name: The Dissipation of Core of Stellarium
1065.DictionaryDes:
The Core of Stellarium within Tina was formed to seal Witch Hruga, condensed from the life-giving power of the Stellarium.

Not meant for ordinary people, Tina's life became tied to it after merging. If it breaks, her Engram would vanish as well.

To save those in the Dark Mist Fortress, the Core of Stellarium within her was depleted and collapsed.
To keep her alive, I used Resonance Power to channel high-concentration Engram from a nearby Hub into her body.

But the collapse of the Core of Stellarium is sure to trigger serious consequences...

1066.Name: About Me - VI
1066.DictionaryDes:
"Sever the source of this calamity, and find your complete self." "Your destination is also your origin."
Connecting this with the previous mysterious revelation, "The eternal calamity is nigh. Awaken, bearer of Resonance Power, remember the sign of salvation, and bear the power of all beings to halt the end of fate."
Airona said that my memories may hold the key to saving this continent: the secret of the source of the Void Engram.

1067.Name: The Sea Lily and Compass Before Departure
1067.DictionaryDes:
To find Hruga's follower, I must temporarily bid farewell to Asterleeds. —parting ways with both Olvera and Tina.

Olvera prepared travel supplies herself, including a sealed glass bottle of Sea Lily , carefully preserved.
Soft white and blue specks, like the pearls and waves of Asterleeds.

Tina gave me her cherished old compass,
one she carried through her early years traveling with caravans.
She said it helped her find her way home when she was lost.

Goodbye, Asterleeds, Sea Lilies, and the wind of freedom.
May we meet again someday...

1068.Name: Hruga's Follower and the Highlands
1068.DictionaryDes:
The person in black, a follower of Hruga, fled with Hruga's Horn to the nearby Bahamar Highlands.
The highlands are a treacherous land full of sand, stone, and crystals, as well as many mysterious ruins left by the Divine.
To uncover my past and unravel the secret of the revelation, I must go to the Bahamar Highlands and find the follower of Hruga.

1069.Name: Golem
1069.DictionaryDes:
The Golem is a rare Bapharian Legacy, known for its immense destructive power and incredible speed. Despite the Divine having departed centuries ago, some of these Golems are still active.
Humanity has had a long history of contact with Golems, but the method to activate them remains unknown, as well as their technological core.
However, Golems can unexpectedly activate when they sense a threat. A rogue Golem usually eliminates all nearby lifeforms, which is why the Council has strictly prohibited private ownership of Golems.

Interestingly, this dangerous characteristic has made Golems even more desirable in the black market. Some wealthy individuals and powerful figures even view owning one privately as a status symbol. The high price of Golems has drawn numerous desperate individuals who form groups, sometimes even bringing along a few artisans, to venture deep into the Divine's ruins just to retrieve an intact Golem and sell it for a hefty profit.

1070.Name: The Election of Council
1070.DictionaryDes:
The council election process in Asterleeds is highly rigorous. Citizens who run for council are called candidates. To become a candidate, one must have made outstanding contributions or shown exceptional performance in their field. And they also need recommendation letters from at least three current councilors to be eligible.

Of course, becoming a candidate is only the beginning. The council conducts strict evaluations on all candidates. Candidates are required to give multiple speeches filled with insight to impress a critical audience-including current councilors, citizens, and even the Council Chair-while clearly outlining their political vision for the future.
At the same time, a background investigation is quietly launched. Council agents will interview and investigate the candidate's living environment, friends and family, and even their neighbors to gain a comprehensive understanding of their true character.

1071.Name: The Scouts of Asterleeds
1071.DictionaryDes:
After the Asterleeds Independence War, although the relationship between Asterleeds and Byrnhald is not one of outright hostility, Asterleeds still maintains great caution.
For example, to avoid surprise attacks, measures such as secretly stationing scouts on the trade routes used for Cargo Delivery in Asterleeds have been implemented.
Asterians cannot suffer the trauma of war again, so they use various methods to carefully avoid it.
This is also the old wound brought by the Asterleeds Independence War.

1072.Name: Andra Landing Battle and the Ecosystem
1072.DictionaryDes:
After the outbreak of the Asterleeds Independence War, Byrnhald troops landed in the Andra Basin from the sea.
In this battle, Byrnhald deployed a giant Golem, determined to take the Andra Basin.
The Asterleeds Defense Corps, led by Olvera, fought back fiercely, and a violent conflict broke out between the two sides, known in history as the Andra Basin Skirmish.
The battle was so brutal that it took many years for the local ecosystem to recover.

1073.Name: The Black Market of Asterleeds
1073.DictionaryDes:
Asterleeds is a city of trade. Besides legitimate business, there are also sizable black markets.
These black markets are usually privately organized and have their own currency and codes.
Wary of the suppression of the Defense Corps, the gathering places and even the members of the black market are not fixed.
The most common goods on the black market are illegal Bapharian Legacies (unauthorized by the Creed), illicit gear made by underground workshops, and goods circulating through unofficial channels.

1074.Name: Rab and Vita
1074.DictionaryDes:
Rab and Vita are two cute cat mascots and the official symbols of the Asterleeds Seabreeze Festival.
Granted the title "Seabreeze Festival Ambassadors," they represent joy and warmth, and deliver blessings.
Their performers are selected by the Council and rigorously trained to ensure quality.

During the festival, residents traditionally exchange letters, and Rab and Vita are the most beloved messengers.
It's said that asking Rab to deliver a love letter greatly boosts your chances of success.

1075.Name: Mutations caused by Engram
1075.DictionaryDes:
In Regnus, Engram flows through every part of the world as the energy that sustains all life.
But when its concentration exceeds the natural balance, strange anomalies emerge:
A turtle's shell may grow uncontrollably and become grotesque.
Fruit may carry deadly toxins. Streams may suddenly boil...

These are not random events, but inevitable signs of the Engram Cycle's collapse.

1076.Name: The Shadows of Asterleeds
1076.DictionaryDes:
After the Independence War, Asterleeds faced a period of unrest.
Brigands appeared outside the city, while thugs and gangs surfaced within.

They caused serious disruption until the Defense Corps cracked down, greatly improving security.
Now, most gangs have moved into the sewers, with only a few still lurking in the shadows.

1077.Name: Webber Energy Network
1077.DictionaryDes:
The Webber Energy Network, built by legendary artisan Webber, powers all of Asterleeds, supporting homes and public infrastructure.

Back when the city was founded, Legacy tech was rarely used, but Webber insisted on converting a Bapharian ruin into an energy hub. His foresight proved correct as usage steadily grew over the decades.

Lately, the network has suffered repeated malfunctions. Some artisans call for a full rebuild, while the Energy Division argues that its modular design just needs upgrades.
Amid the debate, the division's leader mysteriously vanished. Both sides agreed: if upgrades aren't finished on time, the entire system will be replaced.

1078.Name: Energy Division of the Artisan Guild
1078.DictionaryDes:
The Energy Division, founded by the Webber family of Byrnhald, is a special branch of the Asterleeds Artisan Guild, tasked with maintaining the Webber Energy Network.

After the Asterleeds Independence War, while most Byrnhalds left, the division stayed behind. Over time, ideological rifts led to their marginalization. They now rely on self-sufficiency, with rumors of secret support from Byrnhald.
Though officially under the Guild, they operate under Webber Industry, which funds and supports them.

Following their leader's mysterious disappearance, the sewer network began collapsing, triggering an energy crisis. The Council and Guild's silence has made this isolated team the center of the growing threat.

1079.Name: Funerals in the Underground
1079.DictionaryDes:
Funerals in the underground world are twisted and bizarre, unlike anything on the surface.
They begin with a dark dirge, followed by eulogies, then the officiant carves the epitaph into a fence. Assistants and a pharmacist then send the body into a chute.
The pharmacist applies a live-bacteria salve that, upon contact with water, corrodes the body and pushes it through the pipe into the Abyssal, where it fully dissolves as if it never existed.

Such funerals are costly-a rare luxury underground. But what truly makes them exclusive to the wealthy is the unwritten rule: the host inherits all of the deceased's assets, legally and without question.

1080.Name: Scavenger
1080.DictionaryDes:
Across the Asteria Plains, a unique group known as Scavengers roams the wilds with packs on their backs, living apart from the crowded city in their own way.

The wilderness is harsh, so they often travel in small groups. Losing a companion means no one to share fire, offer herbs, or fend off beasts. Mutual support is vital. Though borders aren't formal, each group tends to stick to familiar ground.

Andra Resting Post is their largest gathering spot-a place to sleep, trade goods, and share information.

1081.Name: The Orphanage
1081.DictionaryDes:
The Asterleeds Orphanage was founded by the Creed to support children who lost their parents.

During the Independence War, nearby regions were devastated, leaving many civilians displaced. The Creed set up temporary shelters to ease the suffering. After the war, most returned home-except the orphans.

The Creed then turned the shelters into a permanent orphanage for war orphans, offering strict etiquette training and formal education under the care of its members.

1082.Name: Movements of the Dragons
1082.DictionaryDes:
Decades ago, dragons launched a major invasion of the Duchy of Byrnhald, but were repelled by the Byrnhald Knights and disappeared from sight.

Today, wild dragons are still occasionally seen in remote mountains and wilderness. Though they sometimes clash with locals, their low numbers mean they pose no serious threat.

While dragons are no longer a military concern, their cultural influence remains strong. Dragon-themed stories, plays, and art thrive across the Continent of Magna, and the public still views dragons as powerful and dangerous beings.

1083.Name: Gigantic Automaton
1083.DictionaryDes:
The Gigantic Automatons, mighty war machines forged by the Bapharians, played a crucial role in the war against dragons.
Legend says they were not taken back by the Divine, but lost across the Continent of Magna.
Divine texts state that only chosen individuals can awaken and control them.

1084.Name: Scholars of Asterleeds
1084.DictionaryDes:
In the trade city of Asterleeds, scholars are a rare and often overlooked group, devoted to knowledge and detached from business or politics.
Their abstract research-on topics like the origin of the world or the meaning of life-rarely attracts interest. Asterleeds' pragmatic culture makes it hard for them to survive, and progress often depends on support from the Artisan Guild.

Recently, the Pioneer Bureau's work has highlighted the value of exploring Bapharian Ruins. Some Legacies have had real impact on daily life.
Shrewd merchants, sensing opportunity, have begun funding scholars in hopes their discoveries may spark the next wave of commercial innovation.

1085.Name: Jump Mice-5
1085.DictionaryDes:
An advanced modified Bapharian Legacy with a core module that releases high-pressure gas for instant propulsion.
Perfect for navigating hazardous terrain like mountain ravines.
Originally used by the Byrnhald Knights, it later surfaced on the black market-at a high price.

1086.Name: Secure Chest
1086.DictionaryDes:
An advanced modified Bapharian Legacy, the Secure Chest—also known as a "storage compartment"—is built to hold highly valuable items.
It follows an irreversible protocol: unlocking conditions must be preset, such as a sound frequency, a linked object, or the owner's Bio-Engram signature.

Its outer shell, made of an unknown alloy, can withstand extreme events like volcanic eruptions and earthquakes.
Any forced access while sealed triggers an immediate alarm.

1087.Name: Etched Lines
1087.DictionaryDes:
A glowing, wide stripe often seen in Bapharian Ruins, commonly known among modern artisans as "Etched Lines."

They appear on walls, pillars, and other energy-reliant structures, suggesting they were used by the Divine to transmit power.
Strikingly, even if the structure is damaged, nearby Legacies can still receive energy within a limited range.

1088.Name: Modia's Egg
1088.DictionaryDes:
Legend tells of a Bapharian Legacy called Modia's Egg, said to cure all diseases.
Yet no solid proof has ever surfaced. The Creed denies its existence, and scholars have found no credible records. All known "Modia's Eggs" have been proven to be forgeries.

Some believe it's a black market invention inspired by ancient myths, fueled by people's hope for healing and fascination with lost civilizations. Others claim it exists but was hidden by the Creed due to its forbidden nature.

Even now, many continue to believe that Modia's Egg is real.

1089.Name: City Beneath the Ruins
1089.DictionaryDes:
The ruins are believed to be Andra, a massive city from the Bapharian era. Due to the presence of monsters, the Creed has only set up outposts around its perimeter.

Archaeological evidence suggests it was destroyed during a Bapharian war. But with the Divine's superior technology and supposedly impenetrable defenses-what could have caused its fall?

Recent surveys show that the visible ring structures may only be the upper layers. Most of the city could lie buried underground. The truth might still be hidden below.

1090.Name: Evershade Waters
1090.DictionaryDes:
Evershade Waters is a hazardous maritime zone near Asterleeds, infamous for deadly conditions and frequent shipwrecks.

Its defining feature is a massive whirlpool, hundreds of meters wide. Ships caught in it lose all power and are torn apart, their remains scattered across the sea in a visible debris field.

Due to the constant threat, local sailors avoid the area at all costs.

1091.Name: The Andra Incident
1091.DictionaryDes:
The Andra Incident was a mysterious maritime disaster near Asterleeds.

The cargo ship Andra, operated by the Merchant Guild, went missing in Evershade Waters while fulfilling a contract with the Fishers' Guild. Days later, Captain Dorunik and his crew were found alive, claiming they escaped through a hollow beneath the sea after being pulled into a whirlpool.

The Fishers' Guild doubted their story, suspecting a cover-up for lost cargo, and demanded compensation under the contract terms.

The Merchant Guild denied fault and enlisted the Pioneer Bureau's help. With adventurers' aid, the underwater hollow was confirmed, clearing the crew of all accusations.

1092.Name: Abyssal Eye
1092.DictionaryDes:
The Abyssal Eye is a famed gem across Asterleeds' Seaside regions, especially among sailors and pirates.
Legend says it was forged by dragons and holds the power to destroy entire cities.
But with such power comes misfortune-every known bearer has faced tragedy: monster attacks, loss of loved ones, and total ruin.

1093.Name: Legacy Forging Technology
1093.DictionaryDes:
Forging artisans are one of the four major disciplines within the Artisan Guild, renowned for their mastery of material processing and object creation.
With Legacy forging now widespread, traditional tools like hammers have been fully replaced by Bapharian forging instruments.

Modern artisans can synthesize basic materials into high-grade forging composites, used in ship keels, hardened blades, and precision components. Forging artisans have thus become the cornerstone of the artisan tradition.

1094.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn I
1094.DictionaryDes:
To track down Hruga's Horn, Airona and I came together to the Bahamar Highlands.
This was once the ancient battlefield where humanity fought against Hruga. Legend says her power still lingers on this land, bringing continuous suffering to the people. And now Hruga's Horn has been brought back to this place...

We may not know where to start, but wherever Hruga's Horn is, Engram will surely be distorted. Maybe that's our lead to find Hruga's follower, the person in black.

1095.Name: Illusory Bloom and Illusory Plague I
1095.DictionaryDes:
Illusory Blooms are a crystal-like growth spread across the entire highlands, dazzling to the eye but fraught with peril.
Get too close to a Illusory Bloom and it will overwhelm you, magnifying buried dread and panic in an instant, trapping you in illusion.
Locals refer to the maddening illusions as "Illusory Plague," and steer clear of it in terror.

To my surprise, my body can withstand their illusion-inducing force, yet Airona is completely defenseless against the them...

1096.Name: Pillar of Divinity
1096.DictionaryDes:
Said to be a crucial relic left by the Bapharian Divines, the Pillar of Divinity has now become a holy site of the Creed.
A small number of Creed Guards are stationed near Lallipar to protect the Pillar of Divinity, and they also help locals and pilgrims with everyday troubles.

Notably, all priests serving the Pillar of Divinity come from the Byrnhald Diocese.
High Priest Lothar oversees the site, and the distribution of Starsoul Flasks is handled there as well.

1097.Name: Highland Orange
1097.DictionaryDes:
A common fruit that grows on the highlands. Its bitterness is beloved by ascetics.

After Illusory Plague appeared, villagers who felt unwell or listless would down a big glass of orange juice to perk up.
Because the fruit is extremely bitter, it's usually steamed and sprinkled with salt to soften the bite.

1098.Name: Illusory Bloom Seeds
1098.DictionaryDes:
It's said that even if a Illusory Bloom's crystal shell is shattered, it quickly restores itself—beautiful as a dream, yet a source of endless disaster for the people of the highlands.

At present, the only way to destroy a Illusory Bloom is to purify its Bloom Crystal—what locals call a "seed." Across the entire Bahamar Highlands, only High Priest Lothar of the Creed can perform this purification, and he is also the sole maker of Starsoul Flasks.

1099.Name: Rainbean Rice
1099.DictionaryDes:
Rainbean Rice is Lallipar's signature dish.
Made by steaming aged Rainbeans with rice, their aroma seeps into every grain—rich, fragrant, soft, and comforting, a taste passed down for generations on the highlands.

However, Fafala's Rainbean Rice is nothing like that...
When you crunch a steamed rainbean, its coat is tough without any give, enclosing a mass of crumbs with a texture even poorer than wall ash.
Swallowing it takes not just courage, but an iron throat.

1100.Name: Illusory Bloom and Illusory Plague II
1100.DictionaryDes:
From a chat with Fafala, I heard that thanks to High Priest Lothar's restraint, Illusory Blooms have stayed rare for quite a while.
But recently they've erupted in clusters, even spreading to the village entrance. Many villagers have been hit by Illusory Plague. Something about it feels deeply strange.

1101.Name: Starsoul Flask I
1101.DictionaryDes:
A special antidote used to ease Illusory Plague, outwardly no different from an ordinary glass bottle.
Inside are seven or eight flickering lights—neither dazzling nor holy-looking. Just a faint shimmer, like invisible fireflies.

Since Starsoul Flasks are currently the only way to fight Illusory Plague, they're extremely precious on the highlands. Production is extremely limited, so even money can't buy one among common folk. The Creed distributes Starsoul Flasks to the public on a regular basis to ease Illusory Plague.

The making of Starsoul Flasks depends entirely on High Priest Lothar, the only person on the highlands who can purify Illusory Bloom Crystals.

1102.Name: Hruga and the Illusory Blooms
1102.DictionaryDes:
Hruga was defeated here two hundred years ago. Her power scattered across the land, throwing Engram​ into chaos.
From that moment on, Illusory Blooms began to spread unchecked, triggering countless calamities...

Now, the Void Engrams sealed within Hruga's Horn are disrupting Engram across the highlands like a tangled web—around the northwest river valley, the Bapharian Ruins in the southwest... clogged nodes are everywhere.
And not by coincidence, the recent mass outbreaks of Illusory Blooms seem to be answering that witch's power.

1103.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn II
1103.DictionaryDes:
Hruga has scarred this land far too deeply. Whether it's the spread of Illusory Blooms or the chaos in Engram​, every disaster here is tangled with Hruga's shadow.

So after a brief discussion with Airona, we decided to head northeast to the Sislesa Mine, where Engram is wildly distorted—perhaps Hruga's Horn is hidden there.
Even if we can't pinpoint Hruga's Horn right away, we can at least ease this land's suffering. And maybe follow the trail to the truths being kept hidden.

1104.Name: Sislesa Mine Pass
1104.DictionaryDes:
The Duchy of Byrnhald controls the Sislesa Mine through the Blackstone Legion. Squad members wear black military boots and act with brute force, earning a grim reputation among locals.

Being a military-controlled zone, the mine is currently under martial law. To enter or leave, you need an access pass, but the process is brutally complicated, and usually only merchant groups trusted by the Squad can get one.
But lately, passes have been suspended across the board, with no explanation... Something may have gone wrong inside.

1105.Name: Traveling Troupe I
1105.DictionaryDes:
From the children of Lallipar, we learned that despite the lockdown of the mines, a group called the Traveling Troupe can still pass through and perform in the village.
The parents wouldn't talk about it and kept warning their kids not to watch the troupe's shows. So there's likely something suspect going on.

1106.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn III
1106.DictionaryDes:
With the outpost under martial law, Airona suggested we seek information at the Pillar of Divinity.
Pilgrims from all across the Continent of Magna gather there, and news travels fast among them.

Also, the priests stationed at the Pillar of Divinity belong to the Byrnhald Diocese and have close ties to the Duchy.
If things go well, we might find a way into the mines without forcing our way in. And learn what's really behind the lockdown.

1107.Name: Tales of the Mines
1107.DictionaryDes:
With the Sislesa Mine under martial law and almost no one coming or going, all kinds of ghost stories have sprung up, like these:

The "Don't Look Back" Pebble
"You're working along when suddenly— a pebble flies at you from behind! Whatever you do, don't look back..."

The Mine Tunnel That Appears at Night
"There's a mine tunnel that only shows up at night. Anyone who takes a wrong turn vanishes without a trace. "

Ghost Girl of the Western Highlands
"They say a singing ghost girl has appeared on the West Plateau lately! Late at night, when an adventurer is walking across the plains—suddenly! faint singing drifts from far away..."

These tales are roughly as credible as how much actual meat is in the mine canteen's "meatballs."

1108.Name: Starsoul Flask II
1108.DictionaryDes:
Starsoul Flasks are made primarily from Rockthistle Seeds, which give off a soothing aroma.
The Creed's priests also infuse the seeds with a small amount of Engram, which is why flickering motes appear inside the vial.
It's the gentle nourishment of Engram, paired with Rockthistle's sedative effect, that lets Starsoul Flasks relieve people wounded by Illusory Plague to a certain extent.

But overall, their effects are overstated. Starsoul Flasks are basically a "placebo" and can't truly eradicate Illusory Plague.

1109.Name: Magna's Top Treasure Hunter!
1109.DictionaryDes:
Just as Airona and I were at our wits' end over getting into the mines...
Someone claiming to be "Magna's Top Treasure Hunter" who knew a secret entrance, suddenly appeared.
She claimed that she could help us sneak into the tunnels...
On the condition that we stop holding her theft of the Starsoul Flasks against her.

Her words were hard to trust, and her background was deeply suspicious...
Still, with the lockdown this tight, taking a gamble on her seemed like our only way into the mines.

1110.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn IV
1110.DictionaryDes:
Airona said the most unstable Engram zone lies in the northern Mining Zone.
That area is under heavy restriction. If we barge in, we might meet the Blackstone Legion and drag the miners into trouble, too.
But we have to find Hruga's Horn... we need a careful plan.

1111.Name: Crimson Marrow: Witch's Whisper
1111.DictionaryDes:
A dangerous ore found all through the mine, its surface glowing with a vivid hue much like Illusory Blooms.

The moment you hold it, you can feel an immense surge of Engram​—its purity and sheer volume far beyond imagination. This Engram-rich ore is actually a crystal precipitated from Hruga's Engram fused with Rock Element.

Although there's no obvious immediate effect on people at present, it's baffling that such premium ore hasn't appeared widely in trade.

1112.Name: Illusory Bloom and Illusory Plague III
1112.DictionaryDes:
Once an Illusory Bloom is resonated with, its outer shell collapses and its power source is exposed. The Illusory Bloom is destroyed completely and can never regenerate.

The collapsed core condenses into a crystal much like an Imagine Seed. And even after resonance, it still reeks of Hruga's presence. It's solid proof for the claim that Illusory Bloom are leftovers of Hruga's strength.

The puzzling part is Priest Lothar: by what means does he retrieve the crystals? Through a wondrous Bapharian Legacy, perhaps? Or could it be... that he shares my power to resonate with Illusory Blooms?

1113.Name: Miners in the Mines
1113.DictionaryDes:
Far from the highlands' quiet, measured routine, the Sislesa miners exist in relentless pressure and peril.
They're barred from outside contact and forced to work in mine tunnels thick with Illusory Blooms, soaked in fear day after day.
Every day someone is driven into the most hazardous zones, and every day someone falls beneath the Blackstone Legion's merciless lash.

That's why Darya will go to any lengths to get Starsoul Flasks for them.
Because right now, it's the only thing that can ease their suffering.

1114.Name: Blackstone Legion at the Mines
1114.DictionaryDes:
The Blackstone Legion is the main garrison force overseeing the Sislesa Mine, serving under the Duchy of Byrnhald.

They treat the miners with extreme brutality. All Legion soldiers wear matching black boots, and their march carries a sharp, iron rhythm. Whenever they patrol the mine, the sound of those boots reverberates through the tunnels, sending a chill down every miner's spine.

According to the miners, many of them are from Asterleeds, yet they are not bound to the Squad by any substantive contract.
This odd detail seems to hint at a hidden darkness and disorder within the Blackstone Legion itself.

1115.Name: Isolation Gate of the Transfer Zone
1115.DictionaryDes:
After ore is extracted from the Mining Zone, it is briefly piled here, waiting to be shipped up to the surface.
The area swarms with Blackstone Legion soldiers: dispatchers command by the platforms, laborers haul ore, overseers patrol nonstop, and Squad troops tightly guard every transport route.

The transfer zone is also the Mining Zone's only path to the surface. Once the gate drops, the diggings are cut off completely from the outside world. The gate is built with fortress-like strength, as if the designers always knew that unpredictable dangers were lurking deeper down.

1116.Name: Subterranean Rock Serpent
1116.DictionaryDes:
Rock Serpents are terrifying creatures that lurk deep underground, and they seem to have already built nests within the Mining Zone.
From the soldiers' casual talk, it's clear the Blackstone Legion has been plagued by them for a long time, yet they've never organized a true hunt. Instead, they lower the isolation gate without regard for the miners' safety, crudely sealing the Rock Serpent outside and releasing anesthesia smoke to keep the threat under control.
When the Rock Serpent enters an active phase, the Blackstone Legion even shuts the mines down outright.

As a force serving under the Duchy, the Blackstone Legion should be far from weak. Yet they would rather pay the enormous cost of sealing the mine than take real action.
So what's the truth here? Do they know they're incapable of eradicating the Rock Serpent, but keep mining greedily anyway—trading human lives for ore?
Or has the Squad already fallen into such hidden disorder that they can't even eliminate a single Rock Serpent?

1117.Name: Miner Care Package
1117.DictionaryDes:
We ran into a Rock Serpent outbreak the moment we entered the Mining Zone. Though we barely escaped the tunnels, it meant our plan to infiltrate the area had failed completely.

Darya said she still had a way to get us back into the Mining Zone, but she demanded 500 Starsoul Flasks in exchange, insisting with a resolve we had never seen from her before.
Her fixation may be because she has lost family among the miners... Knowing that these flasks can ease their suffering, we ultimately decided to try gathering that near-impossible number.

After all, only by reaching the Mining Zone can we confirm whether the Engram chaos is caused by Hruga's Horn. And only by uncovering the truth hidden in the Mining Zone might the miners struggling in the dark have a real chance to be truly saved.

1118.Name: Starsoul Flask III
1118.DictionaryDes:
Starsoul Flasks are currently produced entirely by High Priest Lothar alone. He is the only person capable of extracting the crystals, which has made his status in the highlands exceptionally elevated.
However, the recent eruption of Illusory Blooms has drastically increased local demand for Starsoul Flasks, and the already scarce supply has become even more unattainable.

In such a predicament, limiting phial distribution feels more like the Creed's way of easing residents' anxiety than a real solution. To truly resolve the Illusory Plague, we must uncover why the Illusory Blooms are surging and suppress their growth at the source.

1119.Name: Sislesa Mine: Current Situation
1119.DictionaryDes:
Boyce, a noble of the Duchy of Byrnhald, is shockingly unaware of the dire conditions at Sislesa Mine.

According to him, the Blackstone Legion has always opposed the Creed intervening in their internal affairs.
Is this simply the Squad's habitual secrecy and hostility toward outside oversight, or are they afraid that once duchy-aligned priests investigate deeply, the mine's truth will be carried back to Byrnhald and trigger uncontrollable unrest?

Either way, driven by compassion and responsibility, Priest Boyce ultimately decides to bypass the Blackstone Legion's obstruction and investigate the miners in secret. Even at the cost of 500 Starsoul Flasks, he is willing to risk himself to uncover what is really happening.

1120.Name: Business in the Mines
1120.DictionaryDes:
The miners' sunless, underground life has bred countless needs, so merchant caravans stream into the mine in a steady flow.
They mainly sell clothes, fruit, and entertainment goods such as books.
Yet the mine's market is almost entirely monopolized by these caravans, with hardly any independent peddlers. The caravan leader must negotiate with the Blackstone Legion, maintain smooth relations, manage and coordinate the merchants, and issue access permits—most importantly while avoiding interference in the mine's internal affairs.

It is no easy role, and anyone who can shoulder all of this must be extraordinarily shrewd and capable.

1121.Name: Honor Among Thieves! But Not Too Much
1121.DictionaryDes:
Creed Guard Boyce recognizes at a glance that Darya is a "repeat offender"—a self-proclaimed treasure hunter who keeps obtaining Starsoul Flasks through theft and scams, never reforming despite repeated warnings. Darya, focused solely on saving the miners, insists that these are simply her "methods."
Both sides cling to their stance, refusing to yield even an inch.

1122.Name: The Caravan Chief
1122.DictionaryDes:
The argument between Boyce and Darya draws over the caravan leader, Maire. She has already seen through that we are not caravan people, yet calmly proposes a "commission" for us to investigate a traitor within the caravan.
To show her sincerity, she even grants us a "Special Permit" to enter the mine in advance.

Beneath her gentle exterior lies an authority that makes refusal impossible. We understand clearly that if we fail to complete this "commission," she will likely turn around and expose our identities to the Blackstone Legion.

1123.Name: Bekmar Transport Station
1123.DictionaryDes:
To move ore from Sislesa Mine efficiently, the Duchy of Byrnhald set up a dedicated Transport Station.
Some caravans that supply daily necessities to the mine also stop here.
The entire station is under tight Blackstone Legion surveillance. Aside from Squad personnel, only merchants with official passes or special permits may enter or leave.

1124.Name: Special Permit for the Mines
1124.DictionaryDes:
A special permit issued by the Blackstone Legion to certain caravans. Only with this permit can they remain long-term at the Bekmar Transport Station and trade with Squad soldiers.

Its authority is slightly less than a full pass... perhaps Maire didn't want to stir up bigger trouble, so she deliberately gave us a lower-level entry permit. After all, if we're exposed, every merchant here could be dragged into it.

1125.Name: Crimson Marrow: Mining Ban
1125.DictionaryDes:
The reason Crimson Marrow never reached the market is finally clear—the Creed has long issued an explicit ban on mining this dangerous mineral. They've even assigned a dedicated inspector to oversee compliance.

But the reality is, the inspector has no idea the Squad is secretly extracting Crimson Marrow, the clerk does not even know the cargo leaving the hub, and the caravan and soldiers are trading behind everyone's backs.

All these anomalies point to one fact: the Transport Station under Squad control has gone badly off the rails.

1126.Name: The Caravan Traitor Is...
1126.DictionaryDes:
We can now all but confirm the traitor within the caravan is Nevelle.
He's colluding with the Blackstone Legion, planning to eliminate Maire, seize control of the caravan, and smuggle Crimson Marrow.

Even worse, Nevelle may be a pawn of the person in black operating everything from the shadows.

1127.Name: Mustard Tavern
1127.DictionaryDes:
A tavern built against the cliffside, with a strikingly unusual exterior.
It has two levels above and below ground. The upper floor is only open to Byrnhald soldiers and serves as their sole place for leisure.
The lower level is dim and sunless, meant for the miners who can rarely afford to come in.

Whether due to scarce resources or some other reason, the downstairs area has no seating.
Miners can only stack crates outside into makeshift stools, buying themselves a brief rest and a single drink before returning to work.

1128.Name: Astral Stone Mound
1128.DictionaryDes:
After a miner dies, their coworkers stack these stones to honor the dead.

They choose each stone with care, using its texture, shape, and color to sum up a life: jagged uneven stones for passion, small smooth ones for caution, and so on. The piled heap becomes the miners' only tribute to the departed.
Naming it "Astral Stone" may be their way of telling the world that those who rest here once shone like starlight beneath the darkness of the mine.

1129.Name: Commander Klaus's Disappearance
1129.DictionaryDes:
Miners say Commander Klaus of the Blackstone Legion has gone missing, for reasons still unknown.
Since he vanished, Squad patrols have stalled, and shortages have hit everything from rations to medicine.
The Squad has also grown even harsher toward miners, caring only about output. Even wiping sweat can earn a beating.

Meanwhile, a soldier named Carl has risen rapidly after Klaus went missing, quickly seizing influence and acting high and mighty.
This suggests Carl may be colluding with the person in black and has stirred a mutiny within the Blackstone Legion.

1130.Name: Secrets of the Mining Cave
1130.DictionaryDes:
Deep in the extraction tunnels of Sislesa Mine, huge stores of Crimson Marrow are hidden.
Some of it has even been refined and purified. That means the Blackstone Legion isn't just illegally mining at scale—they've set up a secret refining workshop, and even built a carefully concealed passage used solely to transport the Crimson Marrow out.

And this is likely only the tip of the iceberg.

1131.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn V
1131.DictionaryDes:
We found no trace of Hruga's Horn in the Mining zone, yet the person in black we saw earlier remains deeply suspicious.
Crimson Marrow is a residue of Hruga's power and is explicitly banned from mining by the Creed. The connection between the two is enough to suspect that Hruga's followers are pulling strings behind all this.
Whether the person in black is one of Hruga's adherents is still unclear, but if we keep digging, we will unravel the truth thread by thread.

Right now, however, the miners' situation is dire. So we have decided to locate the missing Commander Klaus first—both to curb the illicit mining, and to restore something like normal life to the miners.

1132.Name: The Duchy's Big Shot
1132.DictionaryDes:
With the truth of Mine No. 1 exposed, the identity of the duchy's "big shot" is revealed as well: an inspection commissioner sent by the Duchy of Byrnhald.

The mine's recent lockdown was arranged to receive this VIP, who came to check extraction progress. Yet a notebook found in Mine No. 1 suggests something may have happened to them...
The diary records even darker secrets: "The Crimson Marrow yield plummeted by at least 30% compared to last year. The Drava Royal Institute is barely operational. The duke is furious."

Taken together, it appears the Crimson Marrow mining has long been backed by the Grand Duke of Byrnhald. Bound by the Creed's mining ban, he chose to hide it all in sealed underground operations.

The person in black may have been drawn here by the thick Hruga taint within the ore. Sislesa Mine is now a tangled mess of power, greed, and chaos, and has become a full-fledged breeding ground for evil.

1133.Name: The Truth of Mine No. 1
1133.DictionaryDes:
Mine No. 1 is an abandoned shaft within Sislesa Mine, and it was also the scene of an internal mutiny in the Blackstone Legion.

Our investigation indicates that Adjutant Reiner and his followers launched their rebellion here, suppressing the soldiers who remained loyal to Commander Klaus. Broken blades, bloodstains, and Chaotic footprints left inside the tunnel confirm that a fierce battle took place.

After the incident, Commander Klaus and his devoted subordinate Devin vanished without a trace. Finding them may be the key to uncovering the full truth.

1134.Name: Engram Monitor
1134.DictionaryDes:
To prevent Crimson Marrow from being smuggled out of the mine, the Creed stationed monitors inside the tunnels.
These monitors use a modified piece of Bapharian Legacy to measure the Engram content in extracted ore. If the reading exceeds a set threshold, the device triggers an alarm.

Tests show the Engram Monitors are indeed effective. However, the clues we found suggest that royal researchers from Byrnhald have still been able to access Crimson Marrow, which means there must be some oversight in the mine management.

1135.Name: Waste and Slag
1135.DictionaryDes: In the slang of the Sislesa Mine, "waste material" often refers to Crimson Marrow, which is secretly transported underground for refining. "Slag," on the other hand, is the actual discarded stone with no practical use, usually buried on-site or transported away by vehicles.

1136.Name: Pursuing Hruga's Horn​ VI
1136.DictionaryDes:
By investigating the secret extraction process of Crimson Marrow, we sensed Hruga's presence on Reiner!

Reiner's collusion with the person in black is undeniable. Although the exact purpose of their Crimson Marrow smuggling remains unknown, by continuing to trace the distribution channels, we will uncover the full truth.

1137.Name: Bapharian Trick
1137.DictionaryDes:
Inspection confirms that the Engram Monitor has vulnerabilities—it can temporarily go offline during power fluctuations or maintenance.
The Blackstone Legion exploited this loophole to transport large quantities of Crimson Marrow without leaving a trace— they mixed the Crimson Marrow with ordinary ore and moved it during the monitor's recharge periods.

The monitor set up by the Creed was completely useless. Perhaps the operators were bribed to look the other way, or perhaps they simply neglected their duties.

1138.Name: Crimson Marrow Extraction Process I
1138.DictionaryDes:
Step 1: Mining
In the Mining Zone, miners endure the torment of the Illusory Plague as they work day and night to extract raw Crimson Marrow.
The raw ore contains both Crimson Marrow essence and other mineral materials, and must be processed before it can be used.

1139.Name: Crimson Marrow Extraction Process II
1139.DictionaryDes:
Step 2: Rough Screening
The raw ore is transported to the Bekmar Transport Station for preliminary processing. At the entrance of the smelting furnace, the Squad has installed a hidden diversion device—the raw material rich in Crimson Marrow is secretly separated out, while ordinary ore proceeds into the regular smelting process to become compliant usable ore.

This is what the administrator referred to as "removing prohibited components." But he concealed the truth: what was "removed" was not waste, but illegally mined Crimson Marrow. Instead of being buried as claimed, these contraband materials are secretly sent back underground for further refinement.

1140.Name: Crimson Marrow Extraction Process III
1140.DictionaryDes:
Step 3: Refinement
The diverted raw material rich in Crimson Marrow—labeled as "waste"—is transported through that concealed pipeline directly back to the underground Mining Zone to a secret facility sealed under strict security, inaccessible to ordinary personnel.
In this lightless underground chamber, the ore undergoes deep refinement to extract high-purity Crimson Marrow infused with Hruga Engram​.

The refined product—which Reiner referred to as "waste"—is temporarily stored in a hidden warehouse within the Mining Zone, awaiting the right moment to be smuggled out of the mine.

1141.Name: Forked Mine Paths
1141.DictionaryDes:
Just as our investigation began to show major progress, Darya and Boyce clashed once again...
Darya wanted to lower the isolation gate between the mining tunnels, completely sealing off the Mining Zone from the transfer zone, hoping to free all the miners.
But Boyce believed such drastic action would immediately draw the Squad's attention. If the Squad issued emergency orders and sealed off the mine... the defenseless miners would likely face certain death.

With the situation growing tense, we had no choice but to return to Asterleeds and see whether the Defense Corps soldiers could lend aid to the miners there.

1142.Name: Rab, the Rescuer
1142.DictionaryDes:
Given the complicated war history between Asterleeds and Byrnhald, sending Defense Corps soldiers directly to deal with the highland unrest would only escalate the situation.
But with the miners suffering and a Illusory Bloom outbreak looming, there was no time to hesitate.
So Commander Jerrad devised a plan: disguising himself as a Rab doll, he led a hundred Defense Corps soldiers—posing as "adventurers"—into the highlands to restore order.

1143.Name: Illusory Plague Outbreak...
1143.DictionaryDes:
While we were away, a new and severe Illusory Plague outbreak struck Lallipar.
The villagers have since been fully evacuated, but Airona sharply sensed that this incident might be connected to the person in black.
Our investigation into the smuggling of Crimson Marrow revealed their close ties to the power of Hruga. It is highly likely that the devastating Illusory Plague outbreak in Lallipar was caused by their actions.

1144.Name: Pursuing ​Hruga's Horn​ VII
1144.DictionaryDes:
Airona's investigation showed that all smuggled Crimson Marrow ultimately reached a single "mysterious buyer," with the meeting place located near Lallipar.
Considering the recent Illusory Plague outbreak there... this mysterious buyer is very likely the person in black—and the outbreak itself may not have been an accident at all.

Investigating and resolving the Illusory Plague outbreak in Lallipar may become the key to uncovering the identity and schemes of the person in black.

1145.Name: A Rescue Plan
1145.DictionaryDes:
The highlands' situation has deteriorated further: the Illusory Plague outbreak grows urgent, and the miners underground remain in dire straits.
In the end, we decided to split into two teams:

The first team is in charge of rescuing miners. Jerrad and Airona will lead part of the "adventurers" to infiltrate the Sislesa Mine, opening a supply route and rescuing the trapped miners. The rest of the "adventurers" will gather supplies across the highlands to support Lallipar.

The second team will focus on containing the Illusory Plague. Since I'm the only one capable of purifying Illusory Bloom, I'll remain in Lallipar to suppress the outbreak and assist the Pilgrims. Once the situation is under control, we'll resume our search for the person in black and the whereabouts of Hruga's Horn.

1146.Name: Traveling Troupe II
1146.DictionaryDes:
From the Pilgrims, we learned that the mysterious Traveling Troupe is viewed as outcasts because they do not worship the Bapharian Divines.
Not only that—they avoid bustling Asterleeds and instead wander deep into the remote western highlands, a suspicious behavior that has drawn the Creed's attention.
Rumors even claim they are connected to the Illusory Plague... but is that really the truth?

1147.Name: Rockthistle Seed
1147.DictionaryDes:
The Rockthistle is a pale-yellow flower that blooms alone on sheer cliffs. Its seeds give off a calming scent and are used to craft Starsoul Flasks.

However, while these flasks can soothe the mind to a degree, they cannot cure Illusory Plague at the root. More importantly, Rockthistle Seeds weaken the user's self-control, and overreliance may even dull the mind and lead to mental stagnation.

1148.Name: Gift from Fafala
1148.DictionaryDes:
A cute little handmade doll—a gift from Fafala, a young girl of Lallipar, given during the celebration after we cleansed the giant Illusory Bloom. It symbolizes her hope and trust for the future.

Yet the threat has not truly vanished—the person in black and ​Hruga's Horn​ remain hidden in the shadows.
Perhaps even now, Hruga's followers are lurking in some dark corner, silently watching.
